narrative: Your loan application was carefully reviewed by our credit model, which looks at various aspects of your financial profile to assess the risk of repayment issues, drawing from patterns in historical bank data from 1970s Germany. With your request for 1372 Deutsche Marks to finance a new car over 12 months, and considering details like your existing paid credits, low installment rate of 2% on disposable income, and steady 4-7 years in your current employment as a skilled worker, the model calculated a 41.5% probability that this could turn into a bad credit risk—above our threshold for approval, leading to the denial. The primary factor pushing toward this higher-risk prediction was your checking account status showing less than 0 DM balance, which stands out as a red flag because it suggests limited immediate liquidity compared to many approved applicants who had no account or positive balances. This concern outweighed other positives in the model's eyes. On a brighter note, your short credit duration worked strongly in your favor, as briefer loans like yours are less risky and align better with successful cases than the average 20-month duration among those approved. Similarly, your very low installment rate—well below the 2.94% average for approved applicants—signaled to the model that you'd have plenty of breathing room in your budget, pulling the prediction toward good credit. Overall, while features like your car property, own housing, and lack of other installment plans or guarantors were neutral or typical for approvals, the empty checking account tipped the scales against you, even as a 25-year-old single male foreign worker with just one dependent. To move forward, we recommend building up your checking account balance, perhaps to at least 200 DM or more, and reapplying after a few months to demonstrate stability—many applicants in similar spots succeed this way. You might also consider a slightly longer duration or smaller amount next time to better match approved profiles. We're here to help guide you through strengthening your application.
